title: Horror Devours Oscars 2026: 'Sinners', 'Frankenstein', 'Weapons', and 'KPop Demon Hunters' Stake the Competition
text: The 2026 Oscars marked a significant breakthrough for horror films at the Academy Awards. "Sinners", Ryan Coogler's vampire period piece, claimed Best Original Screenplay along with three other major awards. Horror Film Success: "Sinners" became only the second horror film to win Best Original Screenplay in Academy history, joining Jordan Peele's "Get Out" (2017). The film's dominance at the ceremony included victories in Best Actor (Michael B. Jordan), Best Cinematography (Autumn Durald Arkapaw), and Best Original Score (Ludwig Göransson). Coogler's achievement as a Black screenwriter winning for a horror film represents a historic moment for both the genre and diversity in Hollywood screenwriting. Broader Horror Recognition: Beyond "Sinners", the 2026 Oscars saw strong recognition for horror across multiple categories. Guillermo del Toro's "Frankenstein" won three technical awards: Best Costume Design, Best Makeup and Hairstyling, and Best Production Design. The genre's strong showing at the Oscars represents its best performance since "The Silence of the Lambs" achieved major recognition in 1991. This surge in horror recognition signals a shift in how the Academy values the genre and the artistry within horror filmmaking.
